title: Epicranthes corneri
intro: Epicranthes corneri, also known as the "Madagascar Balsam", is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Madagascar. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, wounds, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Epicranthes corneri include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory infections.
